lecture_rule: A linear curve gives equal roll on both legs
calculation: roll = -slope of log futures price versus time to expiry
trading_implication: A spread needs curvature; slope alone is not enough

lecture_rule: The front/back decision depends on which expiry has the more favorable roll after hedging spot
calculation: Compare local slopes or finite-difference roll by expiry
trading_implication: Preserve any existing simplified sign rule, but validate the sign from the actual curve

calculation: Test whether curvature persists over the planned holding window
trading_implication: The spread can lose even if spot is hedged when the curve shape changes

lecture_rule: g = mu - sigma^2/2; 3x leverage means 3*mu and 9*sigma^2
calculation: Higher variance can make 3x long-run growth lower than 1x
trading_implication: Do not read annual ETF return as simple leverage times index return

lecture_rule: Daily reset creates same-direction trading near the close
calculation: A down day lowers NAV and requires selling to restore target leverage
trading_implication: This is a short-horizon flow signal, separate from the long-run drag math
